page: Amazon Web Services r7iz.8xlarge is a memory-optimized server powered by the Intel Xeon 6455B processor. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it features 32 vCPUs across 16 physical cores with dedicated allocation and a high base clock speed of 3.9 GHz. The system is equipped with 256.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory operating at 5 GHz, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core. It runs on the AWS Nitro hypervisor and includes advanced CPU instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for accelerated matrix operations.

Benchmark data indicates strong memory and processing capabilities. The server achieves top-tier performance in single-core Geekbench testing, as well as single-threaded bzip3 decompression and image processing. Uncached large-block memory read and write bandwidth benchmarks place in the top 25 percent, reflecting the efficiency of the 5 GHz DDR5 memory. Additionally, the hardware demonstrates strong performance in LLM inference text generation for small and medium models, alongside strong static web serving throughput. Multi-core and database operations sit in the average tier.

In terms of resource density, the r7iz.8xlarge offers a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8:1, making it a qualitatively cost-efficient option for workloads requiring massive memory footprints without needing excessive CPU scaling. However, tradeoffs include the complete absence of local NVMe storage and dedicated GPU hardware, meaning all storage must be network-attached and graphics-accelerated tasks are unsupported. Network bandwidth is limited to a baseline of 12 Gbps.

This server is designed for memory-intensive workloads that benefit from high single-core CPU speeds and advanced matrix instructions. Suitable applications include database operations, memory-cached web serving, text and image processing pipelines, and CPU-based low-latency LLM inference.
description: Amazon Web Services r7iz.8xlarge is a memory-optimized server featuring 32 vCPUs and 16 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 6455B processor running at 3.9 GHz. It is equipped with 256.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 5 GHz, delivering an 8:1 memory-to-vCPU ratio. The server utilizes the Nitro hypervisor and supports advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and Intel AMX. Benchmark results show top-tier single-core performance, high image and text processing speeds, and strong uncached memory bandwidth. However, it lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs, relying on a 12 Gbps baseline network interface. This profile makes it highly suitable for memory-intensive database operations, text processing, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r7iz.8xlarge is a memory-optimized server featuring 32 vCPUs, 256 GB of DDR5 RAM, and a 3.9 GHz Intel Xeon processor, designed for high-performance database and memory-intensive workloads.

tagline: A memory-optimized server featuring high-frequency Intel processors and DDR5 memory for demanding database and memory-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: 32 vCPUs and 16 physical cores powered by a 3.9 GHz Intel Xeon 6455B processor
256.0 GB of high-speed 5 GHz DDR5 memory with an 8:1 memory-to-vCPU ratio
Top-tier single-core CPU, text processing, and image processing benchmark performance
Supports Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) and AVX-512 instruction sets
No local storage or GPU hardware, relying on network-attached resources
Best suited for memory-intensive databases, text processing, and CPU-based LLM inference
